title: Lyonia swinhoei
taxonomy: Lyonia swinhoei is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is a shrub native to Taiwan. The plant has been classified in various ways, but it is generally considered a member of the genus Lyonia. Lyonia swinhoei is closely related to other species within the same genus, such as Lyonia ovalifolia and Lyonia ovalifolia var. ovalifolia. Lyonia swinhoei's classification is further supported by its physical characteristics, including its leaves and fruit.
uses: Lyonia swinhoei is a plant species native to Taiwan, but information regarding its medicinal uses is limited. However, traditional Taiwanese medicine has employed various plants, including Lyonia swinhoei, to treat various ailments. properties: Lyonia swinhoei is a species of flowering plant that has been traditionally used in folk medicine in certain regions, particularly in East Asia. constituents: Lyonia swinhoei contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids as its active constituents. These compounds are responsible for its various biological activities. The flavonoids present in Lyonia swinhoei, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The phenolic acids, including ellagic and ferulic acids, contribute to its antimicrobial and anti-cancer properties. The triterpenoids in Lyonia swinhoei have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects.
parts: Lyonia swinhoei is a plant species native to East Asia, and it has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Lyonia swinhoei, also known as the Taiwan lyonia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Lyonia swinhoei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lyonia swinhoei is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is also used in the form of tea or capsules.
